food_truck_application.list_of_food_and_beverages_to_be_sold:
Primary menu (all items available gluten-free on request, with clear allergen labeling at the service window):

Street Tacos:
- Baja Cod Taco – beer-battered Pacific cod, shredded cabbage, cilantro-lime crema, pico de gallo, house salsa roja on a grilled corn tortilla.
- Grilled Sockeye Salmon Taco – BC sockeye salmon, charred corn salsa, avocado, pickled red onion, cilantro, lime wedge.
- Citrus Chicken Taco – marinated chicken thighs (citrus, garlic, oregano), cabbage slaw, chipotle mayo, fresh cilantro.
- Sweet Potato & Black Bean Taco (Vegan) – roasted sweet potato, black beans, corn, cilantro, shredded lettuce, salsa verde.
- Mushroom “Carnitas” Taco (Vegan) – shredded oyster and cremini mushrooms, caramelized onions, pickled jalapeños, avocado crema (vegan).

Bowls:
- Howe Sound Power Bowl – brown rice or quinoa base, citrus chicken or salmon, black beans, roasted veggies, cabbage, salsa, crema.
- Veggie Protein Bowl (Vegan) – quinoa, roasted sweet potatoes, black beans, grilled peppers and onions, pumpkin seeds, salsa verde.

Sides:
- Lime & Sea Salt Tortilla Chips with house-made salsa roja.
- Chips & Guacamole (fresh daily, made with Hass avocados, lime, cilantro, red onion).
- Street Corn Cup (Esquites) – grilled corn off the cob with cotija cheese, chili-lime seasoning (vegan option available).

Kids’ Options:
- Mini Cheese Quesadilla with side of mild salsa.
- Two Mini Chicken Tacos with cheese and lettuce only.

Beverages:
- Bottled still and sparkling water.
- Assorted canned soft drinks (Coke, Diet Coke, Sprite, ginger ale).
- Jarritos (Mandarin, Lime, Guava) when available.
- Fresh Brewed Iced Tea (unsweetened) with optional lemon.
- House Agua Fresca (rotating flavours such as watermelon-lime, cucumber-mint, or hibiscus).
- Locally roasted cold brew coffee (unsweetened, served black; cream/sugar on the side).

Desserts:
- Churro Bites with cinnamon sugar and chocolate dipping sauce.
- Seasonal Fruit Cup (e.g., watermelon, pineapple, berries) – no added sugar.

Dietary and allergen information:
- We clearly mark items containing dairy, gluten, and nuts on our printed and posted menu.
- Most tacos and bowls can be made gluten-free by using corn tortillas and omitting certain sauces.
- We offer vegan options (no animal products) and can prepare them using separate utensils and grill space where practical.
- We keep a detailed ingredient binder on-site that staff can reference for customers with specific allergies.

We focus on using local ingredients where possible (e.g., BC-grown produce in season, locally roasted coffee, and BC seafood suppliers for cod and salmon).
food_truck_application.location_and_description_of_cooking_and_:
All food preparation and cooking will take place inside the self-contained food truck and at our approved off-site commissary kitchen.

On-truck facilities:
- The truck is a 2018 custom-built step van converted into a food truck, outfitted with a commercial kitchen that has been inspected by Vancouver Coastal Health.
- Cooking equipment includes: a 36" flat-top griddle (for tortillas, proteins, and vegetables), a double-basket propane deep fryer (for fish and churro bites), and a two-burner range for sauces.
- There is a certified Type 1 commercial exhaust hood with automatic fire suppression system (wet chemical) covering the griddle and fryer, with regular maintenance logs kept on-board.
- Cold storage is provided by a commercial under-counter refrigerator and a small upright fridge for beverages; all are equipped with integrated thermometers and are checked and logged twice daily.
- Dry storage shelving is secured to the walls and used for non-perishable ingredients and disposable service ware (compostable containers and napkins).
- Hand-washing: one dedicated stainless-steel hand-wash sink with foot pedal controls, paper towels, and liquid soap, located near the service window for easy access.
- Dishwashing: a three-compartment sink system (wash, rinse, sanitize) with appropriate food-grade sanitizer (quaternary ammonium) tested with test strips.
- Water: a 100-gallon potable water tank and a 125-gallon grey water tank, both serviced and emptied at our commissary and approved dump points only. Hot water is provided by an on-demand propane water heater.

Commissary kitchen (off-site):
- We rent a shared, licensed commissary kitchen at: Coastal Commissary Kitchen, 1420 East 3rd Avenue, North Vancouver, BC V7J 1J5.
- At the commissary, we prepare bulk items such as marinated proteins, sauces (salsa roja, salsa verde, cilantro-lime crema), and pre-washed, pre-chopped vegetables. All items are rapidly cooled and stored according to FoodSafe guidelines before being transported in insulated, food-grade containers to the truck each day.
- The commissary has commercial refrigeration, freezers, and additional dry storage, and is inspected regularly by Vancouver Coastal Health.

Food safety protocols:
- All hot foods are cooked to and held at safe temperatures (above 60°C / 140°F) and cold foods are held below 4°C / 40°F. We use probe thermometers and maintain temperature logs during service.
- Cross-contamination prevention: raw proteins are handled on separate color-coded cutting boards and stored in leak-proof containers on the lowest shelves in the fridge. Vegetarian and vegan items are prepped separately wherever possible.
- At the end of each service, all surfaces, equipment, and floors are thoroughly cleaned and sanitized. Any leftover potentially hazardous foods are cooled promptly or discarded according to our written procedures.

All preparation and cooking practices follow FoodSafe Level 1 and Level 2 guidelines, and our staff are trained on these procedures during onboarding and through refreshers at the start of each season.
food_truck_application.any_other_required_approvals_please_list:
- Fire inspection and approval from West Vancouver Fire & Rescue Services completed on 2025-04-10. Documentation attached, including verification of the hood suppression system and portable fire extinguishers (Class K and ABC).
- Valid Mobile Food Vending Permit from the District of West Vancouver for 2025 season (copy attached for reference, recognizing that Lions Bay has its own permitting process).
- Propane system inspection certificate (leak test and regulator check) dated 2025-03-28 from Coastal Gas & Fire Safety Ltd. attached.
- Proof of business registration: Howe Sound Tacos & Bowls registered under BC business number 784329-5. Copy of registration documents attached.
- Copy of FoodSafe Level 1 certificates for all staff and FoodSafe Level 2 for the owner/operator (myself, Jordan Reyes) attached.
food_truck_application.strategies_to_mitigate_impact_on_surroun:
Noise control:
- Our generator is an inverter-style, low-noise Honda EU7000is unit, mounted in a sound-dampening enclosure at the rear of the truck. We position the generator as far as practical from residential properties and orient it so that the exhaust and sound face away from homes and pedestrian gathering areas.
- We do not play amplified music from the truck. The only sound will be normal customer conversation and occasional order calling at a moderate speaking volume. Staff are instructed not to shout orders and to use customer names quietly instead.
- We plan to operate primarily during daytime and early evening hours (11:00–19:00), which reduces late-night noise impacts.

Litter and waste management:
- We will provide clearly labeled waste and recycling bins directly beside the service window: one for general garbage, one for recyclables (bottles and cans), and, where possible, one for compostable packaging and food scraps.
- All take-out containers, cutlery, and napkins used by the truck are compostable or recyclable. We avoid Styrofoam entirely.
- Staff will conduct a litter sweep within a 20–30 meter radius of the truck at least once every hour and again at closing. This includes picking up any stray napkins, cups, or food containers, even if not ours.
- We will transport all garbage and recycling off-site at the end of each day; we will not rely on public bins for bulk waste disposal.

Dust, odor, and smoke control:
- Our cooking is primarily griddle- and fryer-based, with no open-flame grilling that would produce heavy smoke. The exhaust hood has proper filtration and is maintained regularly to minimize odours.
- The frying oil is filtered daily and replaced as needed to prevent burnt-oil smells. Used oil is collected in sealed containers and taken to a licensed recycling facility.
- We will position the truck so that the exhaust vents away from pedestrian seating areas, doorways of nearby buildings, and residential properties, as much as the site layout allows.
- We keep all dry goods and packaging in closed containers or sealed shelving inside the truck to avoid dust from wind or passing vehicles.

Traffic, parking, and neighbourhood fit:
- Staff will arrive in as few vehicles as possible (usually carpooling), and will park in designated areas that do not block driveways, fire lanes, or accessible spaces.
- We will work with the Village to ensure our parking location does not impede sightlines or pedestrian crossings.
- We will encourage customers to line up along the sidewalk or designated area in a single line that does not block building entrances or other vendors.

COVID-19 and general health safety strategies (also useful for general communicable disease control):
- Staff health screening: all staff are instructed not to report to work if they have symptoms consistent with COVID-19, flu, or gastrointestinal illness. We maintain a simple health screening checklist for staff at the start of each shift.
- Hand hygiene: mandatory handwashing for staff at least every 30 minutes, and always after handling money, using the washroom, touching phones, or taking out garbage. Alcohol-based hand sanitizer (minimum 60% alcohol) is available at the service window for customers.
- Surface sanitation: high-touch surfaces (service counter, payment terminal, door handles) are sanitized at least every hour using approved food-safe disinfectants. Food contact surfaces are cleaned and sanitized frequently throughout the day.
- Contactless payment: we strongly encourage tap payments (debit and credit) and mobile wallets. We still accept cash but staff handling cash will not directly handle ready-to-eat food without washing hands and changing gloves first.
- Physical distancing in line: we will post a simple sign at the window asking customers to respect personal space and avoid crowding. When needed, staff will politely remind customers to spread out along the queue.

Community relations and responsiveness:
- We will post a small sign on the truck with our contact email (info@howesoundtacos.ca) and phone number so nearby residents or businesses can reach us directly with concerns about noise, litter, or other impacts.
- If the Village receives complaints related to our operation (e.g., odour or noise), we are committed to adjusting our setup, hours, or procedures in consultation with staff to minimize disruption.

Overall, our goal is to operate as a low-impact, community-friendly food option that keeps the immediate area clean, safe, and welcoming while supporting local residents and visitors with a convenient meal option.
